by_judge.judge: C M POONACHA WILL SIT IN COURT HALL NO.6 AT DHARWAD
S SUNIL DUTT YADAV & VIJAYKUMAR A. PATIL
PRADEEP SINGH YERUR IS NOT HOLDING SITTING ON 05.06.2026.

narrative:
On June 5, 2026, the Karnataka High Court recorded an overall hearing rate of 41.4%, proceeding with 1,034 of the 2,497 scheduled cases across 67 judges. This performance reflects a steady decline in daily clearance rates throughout the week, dropping from 46.2% on June 4, 51.1% on June 3, and 52.3% on June 1. 

Performance among individual benches varied widely. V. Srishananda in Court Hall 21 maintained high efficiency, hearing 67 of 93 scheduled cases (72.0%), while K. V. Aravind achieved a 100% hearing rate across 5 listed matters. S. Vishwajith Shetty managed a high-volume docket in Court Hall 19, hearing 70 of 147 scheduled cases (47.6%). In contrast, M. Nagaprasanna in Court Hall 14 had 305 total cases listed but did not conduct hearings for any of them, and Ashok S. Kinagi cleared only 25 of 114 cases (21.9%) in his primary block.

Procedural stage analysis indicates that "Orders" represented the largest share of the court's workload, with 766 scheduled cases and 402 heard, resulting in a 52.5% conversion rate. Meanwhile, "Final Hearing" stages showed the lowest progress, with only 20.4% of 558 scheduled cases heard. Among case types, Writ Petitions (WP) remained the most frequent, converting 42.7% of 949 scheduled matters. Miscellaneous First Appeals (MFA) converted 48.8% of 369 scheduled cases, whereas Criminal Petitions (CRL.P) experienced significant delay, with only 22.3% of 386 scheduled matters proceeding to a hearing.
